message:
test(lifecycle): assert semantic readiness block has stable shape (ibuuh.11)

ibuuh.11 shape-contract row. The `state.semantic` block in cass
health --json is a stable LLM-contract surface agents parse to decide
whether to wait for semantic catch-up, proceed lexical-only, or
prompt the operator. A silent field rename (fallback_mode → fallback)
would silently degrade every agent's hybrid-planning branch WITHOUT
breaking the wider ilnj9 health golden (which would be intentionally
regenerated when fields are added).

This row asserts the intent directly — each documented field present
with correct type — complementing the byte-level golden. Keeps the
contract preserved while leaving the golden free to absorb additive
changes.

New test semantic_readiness_block_has_expected_shape asserts:
 * string fields: status, availability, summary, fallback_mode,
   preferred_backend, hint
 * bool fields: available, can_search, hnsw_ready, progressive_ready
 * nullable-string fields: embedder_id, vector_index_path, model_dir,
   hnsw_path

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix semantic_readiness_block: 1/1 pass,
5 consecutive isolated re-runs all pass. Full-suite run was 69/69 on
runs 1, 2, 4, 5 and 68/69 once due to a pre-existing flake in
concurrent_health_readings_agree (row 1) under parallel test-pressure.
That row's flake is unrelated to this commit — my new test is stable.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.11.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
